steps.title: Identify the research arc
steps.content: The passage presents the structure of a scientific study: it introduces researchers and their question (how wooden shipwrecks affect seafloor microbial communities), describes their methodology (placing wood samples at different distances from shipwrecks to collect samples), and reports their findings (wood type had a larger effect on bacteria than on archaea or fungi). This progression from question to method to result is the defining framework of a study overview.

steps.title: Match the structure to the choices
steps.content: Choice D is correct because it names exactly what the passage does—provide an overview of a study about wooden shipwrecks' effects on microbial communities. The other three choices misidentify the passage: A introduces a focus on fish (which the passage never discusses), B frames it as a discovery narrative (which it isn't), and C treats an opening reference as the primary subject. That matches choice D — D is correct.
